comments.author: @maidsua4208
comments.text:
The country has every reason to be proud of what they achieved during the World Cup. I come from a small country of 5.5 million that will play a match against a nation of 213 million, Norway-Brazil. Brazil will most likely win, but I am proud of what our small country has achieved.

Curacao is as beautiful as I imagined, I would have loved to visit this small but beautiful country. Make sure you keep your identity and don't get ruined by mass tourism.

replies.author: @afcansf5996
replies.text: Or you stop talking about things you have no clue about it, since you clearly don''t know The Netherlands has delt with it's past (as far that's possilbe). Not to mention they and the other islands were given the chance to become fully indepedent in referendum. And all of the islands decided to stay. Whether clowns like you want to believe it or not, they and the other islands are better of being part of the Kingdom of The Netherlands. Having the same status and being what Scotland, Wales & Northern Ireland are to the UK. And all the cultural, economical, educational, financial, medical, foreign relations/treaties, legal & military security etc that comes with being part of The Netherlands; and by extention the EU. So why don't you take a hike with your fake cconcern & outrage.

comments.author: @americankickboxer
comments.text: I am a FIFA WC volunteer at the Fan Fest in Houston. The day before the game against Germany, it seemed like the entire nation of Curacao was there standing in line to get in to see a band from Curacao play. They were all so happy and fun and nice, didn't mind standing in the sun and waiting very patiently for the gates to open. I was rooting for them the entire tournament. Now I need to visit.
